titleStmt.title: SII 40.249: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 249. (A.R. No. B. 294 of 1933.) Mādala, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a pillar set up in the compound of the Sakalēśvarasvāmi temple.
This is dated Śaka 1075, Uttarāyaṇa Saṅkrānti.

Records the gift of 55 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Sakalēśvaradēva by Kommaka, the daughter of Prōlaya of Prāṁbaṟu, who was a favourite servant of the king. The gift was entrusted to Nāre, son of Yeṟayanābōya for supply of ghee to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1075 gunēṁṭṭi Uttarā
yana saṁkrāṁtti nimityamuna śrīmanmahā
maṁḍalēśvara velanāṭi kulōttuṁggacōḍago
ṁkkarājulaku niṣṭabhrityuṁḍaina prāṁbaṟṟi prō
laya kūṁttuṟu kommaka tana tallidaṁḍrulaku dha
rmmamugā sakalēśvaradēvarakakhaṇḍavatti dīpaṁbuna
kiccina goṟiya 55 vīni jēkoni yeṟayanābōyi
nākoḍku nāre dana puttrānupautrika mācaṁdrārkkamu
dīpaṁbunaku nityamāneṇḍu nēyi voyanalavāṇḍu
Digital edition of SII 40.249 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
